jokes.text: "Be it decreed: Should the taxation of tea rise to an unacceptable level, citizens shall dump Ted into Ramsett Lake."
jokes.setup: Reading what appears to be a serious historical charter
jokes.punchline: The charter mandates throwing a person named Ted into a lake instead of tea
jokes.explanation: Clever premise that subverts expectations about historical documents. The absurdity is well-constructed and creates the episode's central joke.

jokes.text: "Historical fun fact, when the Founding Fathers wrote the charter, their old-timey script made one of the As look like a D, so 'tea' became 'ted.'"
jokes.setup: Explaining the historical origin of the tradition
jokes.punchline: It's all based on a simple handwriting error
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurdity. Leslie's earnest delivery of obviously ridiculous 'history' is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: "I don't even go by Ted! I go by Theodore!"
jokes.setup: Ted is being counted down to being thrown in the lake
jokes.punchline: His desperate protest about his preferred name in the face of ritualized dunking
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment. Ted's futile insistence on his formal name while about to be dunked is appropriately pathetic.

jokes.text: "You were so funny when you were like, 'My cell phone, I left it in my pocket.' Then you checked it, remember, and it didn't work."
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to console Ted after the dunking
jokes.punchline: Her tone-deaf recounting of his misfortune as if it were intentional comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her obliviousness to how inappropriate her cheerfulness is. Good character integration.

jokes.text: "Ha! Classic Ted."
jokes.setup: Ted seriously says he's done with Ted Party Day
jokes.punchline: Leslie dismisses his legitimate complaint as if it's a character quirk
jokes.explanation: Economical and perfectly captures Leslie's dismissive optimism. The brevity makes it punchy.

jokes.text: "Here's a quarter. I own your car now. The Pawnee Charter clearly states that any white citizen has the right to seize any Indian property for 25 cents."
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to enforce every old law
jokes.punchline: Ted immediately finds a racist law to demonstrate the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that exposes the flaw in Leslie's argument. Dark but earned, and structurally perfect as an immediate counterargument.

jokes.text: "Whoa, I think we should be careful when we speculate what the Founders intended when they wrote the charter."
jokes.setup: Leslie objects to the racist law interpretation
jokes.punchline: Ted throws her exact earlier words back at her
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback that turns Leslie's logic against her. The structure of the argument reversal is well-executed.

jokes.text: Ted slaps Leslie based on an 1868 statute about women raising their voices to landowning males
jokes.setup: Leslie raises her voice to Ted
jokes.punchline: Ted slaps her citing another archaic law
jokes.explanation: Bold physical escalation that drives home the point about absurd old laws. The visual shock value works well.

jokes.text: "I was not aware of that statute."
jokes.setup: Leslie has just been slapped based on an old law
jokes.punchline: Her understated, almost academic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie reaction - treating the slap like a research oversight rather than an assault. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: "I'd work all night if it meant nothing got done."
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his philosophy about helping April avoid work
jokes.punchline: The paradox of working hard to achieve nothing
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Ron Swanson line. Perfect encapsulation of his anti-government philosophy with beautiful logical paradox.

jokes.text: literally my two favorite people in the cataloged universe
jokes.setup: Chris expressing enthusiasm about Ron and April
jokes.punchline: The overly specific and grandiose 'cataloged universe'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris Traeger over-enthusiasm. The wordplay is typical but fits the character perfectly.

jokes.text: "Ron, you've never been CTMTSed?"
jokes.setup: Chris creating an acronym for his seminar
jokes.punchline: Using the acronym as a verb in the most awkward way possible
jokes.explanation: Good Chris moment showing his corporate speak obsession. The verbification of the clunky acronym is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: "It better not be Terrence Howard's tank top from Hustle & Flow."
jokes.setup: Ann warning Ben about what he's bidding on
jokes.punchline: The extremely specific and random celebrity memorabilia
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific reference that's funny for its randomness, but doesn't integrate well with Ann's character.

jokes.text: "'Cause I am bidding on it and I am ready to spend an amount that my accountant calls 'dangerous and irresponsible.'"
jokes.setup: Ann's warning about the tank top
jokes.punchline: Revealing her own obsessive bidding with accountant's worried assessment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ann's unexpected intensity. The accountant's quote is a nice specific detail.

jokes.text: Ben's calendar showing Leslie's absurd number of anniversaries including 'Talk Like A Pittsburgh Pirate day'
jokes.setup: Ben explaining Leslie's many anniversaries
jokes.punchline: The visual of the completely packed calendar with increasingly specific celebrations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character detail. The Pittsburgh Pirate specification is absurdly specific and shows her over-the-top nature.

jokes.text: "It's like being smothered with a hand-quilted pillow filled with cherished memories."
jokes.setup: Ben complaining about Leslie's thoughtfulness
jokes.punchline: The poetic description of being overwhelmed by her caring nature
jokes.explanation: Beautiful metaphor that captures both the sweetness and suffocating nature of Leslie's attention. Great character writing for Ben.

jokes.text: "Hell yeah. Have you seen those Dothraki dudes? They can get it. Everybody on that show can get it."
jokes.setup: Ben is surprised Ann watches Game of Thrones
jokes.punchline: Ann's unexpectedly thirsty commentary about the attractive cast
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion showing Ann's hidden lustful side. The repetition of 'can get it' escalates nicely.

jokes.text: Calendar Day present celebrating the first time she ever bought me a calendar
jokes.setup: Showing Leslie's anniversary calendar
jokes.punchline: There's an anniversary for when she first bought him the calendar to track anniversaries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recursive joke - the meta-nature of celebrating the creation of the calendar itself shows Leslie's obsession perfectly.

jokes.text: "My name is Garth Blundin, and I object to your ridiculous proposal. Uh, the Pawnee Charter shall not be changed. Not today, not ever!"
jokes.setup: Public comment period opens
jokes.punchline: Immediate passionate objection with dramatic flourish
jokes.explanation: Sets up Garth as an over-the-top opposition character. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This is why we don't hang out
jokes.setup: Ann getting intense about Game of Thrones
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt assessment of their friendship
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their established dynamic. Ben's matter-of-fact delivery makes it land well.

jokes.text: Garth beginning his Star Wars filibuster: "Pan down from the twin suns of Tatooine..."
jokes.setup: Garth invokes filibuster rule to prevent voting
jokes.punchline: He launches into detailed Star Wars fan fiction instead of political speech
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of political filibuster expectations. The detailed fan fiction is perfectly in character for a basement-dwelling nerd.

jokes.text: "Imagine those two going up against Robot Chewbacca, 'cause that's gonna happen."
jokes.setup: Garth's continued Star Wars/Marvel crossover pitch
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact certainty about Robot Chewbacca
jokes.explanation: The confident delivery of 'that's gonna happen' about Robot Chewbacca is perfect fan delusion. Good economy.

jokes.text: "By that logic, every time I go to bed at night, I am Wolverine."
jokes.setup: Leslie's claim about putting on historical costumes
jokes.punchline: Garth's comparison to his X-Men pajamas
jokes.explanation: Great logic and perfect character moment. The image of adult Garth in X-Men pajamas is both pathetic and endearing.

jokes.text: "You bought X-Men pajamas?" "I won them, madam, in a raffle."
jokes.setup: Leslie's incredulous question about the pajamas
jokes.punchline: Garth's dignified clarification about winning them in a raffle
jokes.explanation: The formal 'madam' combined with raffle pajamas is perfect character detail. Shows his dignity about ridiculous things.

jokes.text: they also had wooden teeth and pooped in holes in the ground
jokes.setup: Arguing the founding fathers were brilliant and courageous
jokes.punchline: But also primitive in basic hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good reality check humor. The juxtaposition of reverence with gross historical facts lands well.

jokes.text: "Hop on board The Management Train. First stop, Motivation Station. All aboard!"
jokes.setup: Chris beginning his management seminar
jokes.punchline: The aggressively cheerful corporate training speak
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment but the corporate training parody is fairly predictable. Good character integration saves it.

jokes.text: "You're 'Future Mrs. Tiger Woods'? I made that profile, like, ten years ago. I don't know how to change it."
jokes.setup: Ben discovering Ann's eBay username
jokes.punchline: Her embarrassed explanation about the outdated aspirational username
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's past optimism about Tiger Woods. The helplessness about changing it is relatable.

jokes.text: "The Lannisters, while very wealthy, do not possess the magical abilities of, say, the Warlocks of Qarth for example."
jokes.setup: Ben makes incorrect Game of Thrones reference
jokes.punchline: Ann's detailed correction showing her deep fandom
jokes.explanation: Continues the Ann/Game of Thrones character development. The specificity shows her unexpected nerd credentials.

jokes.text: "First of all, you don't steer a locomotive. The tracks do."
jokes.setup: Chris's locomotive metaphor for management
jokes.punchline: Ron's literal and practical correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - practical, literal, and deflating corporate metaphors. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: "Money, fear, and hunger."
jokes.setup: Ron listing ways to motivate people
jokes.punchline: The brutally simple and dark assessment
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Ron philosophy delivered with perfect economy. Dark but true, and completely character-consistent.

jokes.text: "When people feel supported, they will literally explode with productivity."
jokes.setup: Chris countering Ron's dark motivation theory
jokes.punchline: His earnest use of 'literally' for obvious metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character moment with his 'literally' misuse, but the joke is fairly predictable for his character.

jokes.text: "My spirit blood is on your hands."
jokes.setup: April claiming Chris killed her spirit
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous concept of 'spirit blood'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April weirdness. 'Spirit blood' is beautifully absurd and delivered with her characteristic deadpan intensity.

jokes.text: "I don't want to seem overdramatic, but this is literally a battle for April's soul."
jokes.setup: Chris setting up the management challenge
jokes.punchline: Claiming not to be overdramatic while being extremely overdramatic
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure with Chris's complete lack of self-awareness about his drama level.

jokes.text: "I don't want to seem overdramatic, but I don't really care what happens here."
jokes.setup: April echoing Chris's 'don't want to seem overdramatic' phrasing
jokes.punchline: Her complete disinterest delivered in the same format
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure with perfect April deadpan. The parallel construction makes Chris's intensity seem even more ridiculous.

jokes.text: Old things are dumb. It's like, just be new.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about the historical house
jokes.punchline: Reductive command to inanimate objects to 'just be new'
jokes.explanation: Peak Tom shallow materialism. The simple command 'just be new' captures his entire worldview perfectly.

jokes.text: Already, I've done the preserves and chased off an interloping cable installer
jokes.setup: Garth showing off his historical living commitment
jokes.punchline: Mix of period-appropriate and modern concerns with pretentious vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Garth's pretentious vocabulary ('interloping') with practical historical cosplay commitment.

jokes.text: "Definitely didn't volunteer. I had much better things to do. Leslie said she'd pay me 100 bucks."
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his role as referee
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have better things to do while clearly being bribed to be there
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his mercenary nature while pretending to be busy and important.

jokes.text: "And I had nothing better to do. I'm doing it for free."
jokes.setup: Following Tom's elaborate excuse
jokes.punchline: Andy's honest and simple admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy honesty contrasting with Tom's pretense. The simplicity is refreshing and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: "Hello, Ben. Seems like I love Leslie more than you do."
jokes.setup: Introduction of the mailwoman character
jokes.punchline: Her immediate competitive claim about loving Leslie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the waffle iron competition. The directness of the love challenge is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Say no more. Here's a bunch of guns.
jokes.setup: Ben asking Ron for help
jokes.punchline: Ron's default solution being weapons
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron response - assuming any problem can be solved with firearms. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: "Let's start the bidding at $650,000."
jokes.setup: Setting up the bidding war for the waffle iron
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously high starting bid for a used waffle iron
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation showing how the competition has gotten out of hand. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: "I'll give it to you if you go on a date with me." "Oh, God. I can't believe I'm about to do this, but fine."
jokes.setup: Pawn shop owner's inappropriate proposition
jokes.punchline: Ann's disgusted but desperate acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how far the competition has gone. Ann's reluctant acceptance is appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I'll give it to you if you go on a date with me
jokes.setup: Multiple people bidding increasingly ridiculous amounts
jokes.punchline: Sleazy proposition instead of money
jokes.explanation: Weak sleazy guy humor. Predictable and relies on uncomfortable gender dynamics without clever execution.

jokes.text: "One time my refrigerator stopped working, I didn't know what to do. I just moved."
jokes.setup: Tom commenting on the historical living conditions
jokes.punchline: His extreme solution to a simple appliance problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment showing his impractical approach to life. The escalation from broken fridge to moving is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: "All my favorite foods have butter on 'em. Pancakes, toast, popcorn, grapes. Butter is my favorite food."
jokes.setup: Andy commenting on the butter churning
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd list ending with pure butter preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment. The list structure builds nicely with 'grapes' being unexpected, then 'butter is my favorite food' as the perfect cap.

jokes.text: How long was that under there?
jokes.setup: Something gross being discovered
jokes.punchline: Andy's disgusted reaction to mysterious old food/object
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor. The visual gag works but isn't particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: The earthquake that destroys part of the historical house
jokes.setup: The historical living competition has been going on
jokes.punchline: An actual earthquake interrupts their period-accurate experience
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that literally breaks up the competition. The timing is well-executed as a game-changing moment.

jokes.text: Look at my hoop, Leslie. Look at my hoop!
jokes.setup: Tom trying to enjoy historical activities
jokes.punchline: Desperate enthusiasm for simple colonial entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good contrast with Tom's usual sophisticated persona. His desperate excitement about a simple hoop shows good character work.

jokes.text: Ron's motivational experiment with Jerry: "You are smart and capable. And I believe you can accomplish anything."
jokes.setup: Ron testing positive motivation on Jerry
jokes.punchline: Ron delivering completely uncharacteristic encouragement
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion seeing Ron try Chris's methods. The discomfort in giving encouragement is palpable.

jokes.text: The only ones he gets are spam. And he responds to them.
jokes.setup: Looking at Garth's email history
jokes.punchline: Not only does he have no friends, he engages with spam
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of loneliness - spam is the only 'communication' he gets, and he treats it as real. Dark but well-crafted.

jokes.text: "Oh, my God. It's true. He hasn't gotten an email in twelve days! Is he a ghost?"
jokes.setup: Leslie checking Garth's phone for cheating
jokes.punchline: Her horror at discovering his complete social isolation
jokes.explanation: Good revelation about Garth's pathetic isolation. 'Is he a ghost?' is a nice escalation of the disbelief.

jokes.text: "The only ones he gets are spam. And he responds to them."
jokes.setup: Continuing the exploration of Garth's email situation
jokes.punchline: The pathetic detail of him responding to spam
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Garth's sadness. The image of him earnestly responding to spam is both funny and tragic.

jokes.text: "A birthing stick." "What's that?" "You do not want to know."
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about Garth's whittling project
jokes.punchline: The ominous name and Garth's warning against learning more
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor about historical realities. The warning makes it funnier by letting imaginations run wild.

jokes.text: "Yes, I will go out on a date with you, but I realize you're a modern woman, so I will let you pay."
jokes.setup: Garth misinterpreting Leslie's proposition
jokes.punchline: His backwards 'feminist' gesture about letting her pay
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Garth's backwards understanding of gender equality. The misplaced chivalry is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Yes, I will go out on a date with you, but I realize you're a modern woman, so I will let you pay
jokes.setup: Leslie making another proposition
jokes.punchline: Garth's 'progressive' offer to let her pay for their date
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Garth's warped understanding of modern gender equality. The backwards 'progressiveness' works well.

jokes.text: Huzzah, I'm Ted! I'm Ted! I'm Ted, too, guys.
jokes.setup: Volunteers representing Ted in the lake dunking
jokes.punchline: Everyone enthusiastically claiming to be Ted
jokes.explanation: Good resolution to the Ted Party Day plot. The enthusiasm for being dunked shows good character development.

jokes.text: "Huzzah, I'm Ted! I'm Ted! I'm Ted, too, guys. Andy! Yeah! He's naked, everybody! Put that away! We're all Ted! Yeah! No, Andy!"
jokes.setup: The Ted Party Day celebration with volunteers
jokes.punchline: Andy getting naked and the chaos that ensues
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy chaos moment. The escalation from celebration to nudity panic is appropriately chaotic.

jokes.text: "Thank God. I don't know how to swim."
jokes.setup: Ted expressing relief about not being thrown in the lake anymore
jokes.punchline: Revealing he can't swim, making the tradition even worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect final revelation making the Ted Party tradition even more cruel. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: I will pit Ron and Chris against each other. They'll argue about dumb stuff that doesn't matter, and I will bail. Then I'll steal $20 from Chris's wallet and buy pizza with Andy.
jokes.setup: Revealing April's written seminar goal
jokes.punchline: Her manipulation plan and petty theft goal
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor revealing April orchestrated the entire conflict. Perfect character integration with her manipulative nature.

jokes.text: April's note: "I will pit Ron and Chris against each other. They'll argue about dumb stuff that doesn't matter, and I will bail. Then I'll steal $20 from Chris's wallet and buy pizza with Andy. Oh. and I'll also steal Ron's watch, just for fun."
jokes.setup: The reveal of April's seminar goals
jokes.punchline: Her complete manipulation of both Ron and Chris for personal gain
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta joke that recontextualizes the entire subplot. April's manipulative intelligence and petty theft 'just for fun' is perfect character work.

jokes.text: "Wait, what? Oh, my God. You're both dying."
jokes.setup: Ann and Ben saying they won't celebrate certain holidays anymore
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate assumption they're terminally ill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie reaction showing her inability to imagine anyone not wanting to celebrate holidays with her unless they're dying.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You're both dying.
jokes.setup: Ben and Ann saying they won't celebrate those holidays anymore
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate assumption they're terminally ill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie catastrophic thinking. Her immediate jump to terminal illness shows her dramatic personality perfectly.

jokes.text: That's ridiculous, I'm so much better.
jokes.setup: Being told she's no better at anniversaries than others
jokes.punchline: Immediately proving the point by bragging about her superiority
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie self-awareness failure. The immediate contradiction shows her competitive nature overriding her humility.
